sources.content:
"""
Problem 009 -- expert_token_scatter_with_weighted_forward (BACKWARD), B200.

Numerics are unchanged from the proven 0.721 parent kernel: G2 and the two
K=N wgrads G3/G5 run in TF32 (fp32 operands, 10-bit mantissa, fp32 output --
this is what avoids the bf16 K=N wgrad rounding wall), G1/G4/G6 stay
bf16/fp32-accum (already safe). The new lever is launch-overhead collapse via
CUDA graphs: 13 of 16 graded shapes are memory/launch-bound (weight floor
~0.10ms) with 15+ small eager kernel launches (gather/cast/mul/scatter/GEMM)
competing for that same budget -- sub-10us launches are overhead-dominated
(see kb/benchmarking-discipline.md), and this parent kernel issues them all
eagerly with no graph at all.

Design: capture everything EXCEPT the three big-output wgrad GEMMs (G1, G3,
G5) into one CUDA graph per (B, N) shape. Returning a CUDA-graph static
buffer requires clone()-ing it first (never alias a buffer a later replay
will overwrite -- see kb correctness-gating notes), and G1/G3/G5 write the
three ~117MB grad_w{1,2,3}_weight outputs directly; cloning ~350MB every call
would roughly double the fixed 704MB weight-floor traffic and net-lose. So
G1/G3/G5 stay eager (a fresh allocation per call needs no clone) reading
stable graph-owned intermediates (geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32) --
safe because a graph's output buffers remain valid, ordinary tensors once
replay() has been issued on the same stream. Only the two remaining outputs
(grad_hidden_states, grad_routing_weights) are graph-owned and get clone()'d;
both are cheap relative to the weight floor.

The graph is captured once per (B, N, H, F) shape (all 16 graded shapes are
distinct, so no key collisions) and replayed whenever the next call's input
pointers still match capture time -- true within one workload's warmup+timed
loop per kb/sol-execbench-eval-loop-memoization.md. Any pointer mismatch, or
a capture failure of any kind, transparently falls back to the exact eager
computation (mathematically identical to the proven 0.721 kernel, just
regrouped across two helper functions), so this change can only add speed,
never regress correctness below the parent.
"""

import torch

torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True

_graph_cache = {}


def _graph_forward(
    grad_output,
    token_indices,
    selected_tokens,
    w1_output,
    gate_output,
    up_output,
    gated_output,
    expert_output,
    selected_weights,
    w1_weight,
    w2_weight,
    w3_weight,
):
    """Everything except the three big wgrad GEMMs (G1/G3/G5). Only touches
    the 12 stable external inputs plus tensors it allocates itself, so it is
    safe to run inside a CUDA graph capture."""
    B, H = grad_output.shape
    device = grad_output.device

    gwo = grad_output.index_select(0, token_indices)  # (N, H) bf16

    grad_sel_w = (gwo.float() * expert_output.float()).sum(dim=-1)
    grad_routing_weights = torch.zeros(B, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_routing_weights[token_indices] = grad_sel_w.to(torch.bfloat16)

    selected_weights_f = selected_weights.float()
    geo_bf16 = gwo * selected_weights.unsqueeze(-1)            # (N, H) bf16
    geo_f32 = gwo.float() * selected_weights_f.unsqueeze(-1)   # (N, H) fp32

    # G2: grad_gated = geo @ w2_weight -- TF32 so grad_gated keeps full
    # precision for the downstream K=N wgrads G3/G5.
    w2_f32 = w2_weight.float()
    grad_gated_f32 = torch.matmul(geo_f32, w2_f32)  # (N, F) fp32

    up_f32 = up_output.float()
    gate_f32 = gate_output.float()
    w1_f32 = w1_output.float()

    sig = torch.sigmoid(w1_f32)
    silu_grad = sig * (1.0 + w1_f32 * (1.0 - sig))

    grad_up_f32 = grad_gated_f32 * gate_f32
    # Match reference evaluation order: (grad_gated * up_output) * silu_grad.
    grad_w1o_f32 = grad_gated_f32 * up_f32 * silu_grad

    # G4/G6 dgrads -- bf16/fp32-accum is safe for the per-token output.
    grad_up = grad_up_f32.to(torch.bfloat16)
    grad_w1o = grad_w1o_f32.to(torch.bfloat16)
    gst_w3 = torch.matmul(grad_up, w3_weight)
    gst_w1 = torch.matmul(grad_w1o, w1_weight)

    grad_selected_tokens = gst_w1 + gst_w3
    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros(B, H, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_hidden_states[token_indices] = grad_selected_tokens

    return geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32, grad_routing_weights, grad_hidden_states


def _finish(geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32, gated_output, selected_tokens):
    """The three big-output wgrad GEMMs (G1/G3/G5). Always eager so every
    call gets a fresh, non-aliased output buffer -- no clone required."""
    grad_w2_weight = torch.matmul(
        geo_bf16.transpose(0, 1), gated_output
    ).to(torch.bfloat16)

    selected_f32 = selected_tokens.float()
    grad_w3_weight = torch.matmul(
        grad_up_f32.transpose(0, 1), selected_f32
    ).to(torch.bfloat16)
    grad_w1_weight = torch.matmul(
        grad_w1o_f32.transpose(0, 1), selected_f32
    ).to(torch.bfloat16)

    return grad_w1_weight, grad_w2_weight, grad_w3_weight


def _build_graph(key, args):
    """Capture _graph_forward for this (B, N, H, F) shape. Raises on any
    failure -- the caller guards it, keeping try/except out of `run`."""
    side_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()
    side_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
    with torch.cuda.stream(side_stream):
        for _ in range(3):
            _graph_forward(*args)
    torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_stream(side_stream)

    graph = torch.cuda.CUDAGraph()
    with torch.cuda.graph(graph):
        outputs = _graph_forward(*args)

    _graph_cache[key] = {
        "graph": graph,
        "ptrs": tuple(t.data_ptr() for t in args),
        "outputs": outputs,
    }


def _try_capture(key, args):
    try:
        _build_graph(key, args)
    except Exception:
        _graph_cache[key] = None


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    """
    Backward pass for expert token scatter with weighted forward.

    Returns (all bfloat16):
      grad_hidden_states (B, H)
      grad_routing_weights (B,)
      grad_w1_weight (F, H)
      grad_w2_weight (H, F)
      grad_w3_weight (F, H)
    """
    B, H = grad_output.shape
    N = token_indices.shape[0]
    F = w1_weight.shape[0]
    key = (B, N, H, F)

    args = (
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
    ptrs = tuple(t.data_ptr() for t in args)

    if key not in _graph_cache and grad_output.is_cuda:
        _try_capture(key, args)

    entry = _graph_cache.get(key)

    if entry is not None and entry["ptrs"] == ptrs:
        entry["graph"].replay()
        geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32, static_routing, static_hidden = entry["outputs"]
        grad_routing_weights = static_routing.clone()
        grad_hidden_states = static_hidden.clone()
    else:
        geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32, grad_routing_weights, grad_hidden_states = _graph_forward(*args)

    grad_w1_weight, grad_w2_weight, grad_w3_weight = _finish(
        geo_bf16, grad_up_f32, grad_w1o_f32, gated_output, selected_tokens
    )

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )

"""

K=N wgrads G3/G5 run in TF32 (fp32 operands, 10-bit mantissa, fp32 output --
